rag_rules: Place assume/guarantee declarations in component type scopes for interface behavior; use implementation scopes for assign bindings.
Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks to contain AGREE contract elements.
generic_rules: Refer to component ports only through valid variables or outputs in type-level contracts.
Declare constants and equations in component type annexes when describing interface behavior.
Use visible identifiers from the component's feature set in AGREE expressions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE syntax includes `assume "label": expression;`, `guarantee "label": expression;`, `eq name : type = expression;`, and `const name : type = value;`.
Assignments are placed in implementation scopes using `assign output = expression;`.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes.
Avoid referencing AADL properties like Compute_Execution_Time as AGREE variables.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and their automatic scope in AGREE expressions
Model fusion and architecture redesign during repair
